km.Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England: អាsតុន បូថារeល, shរ៉ុពស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾阿斯頓 波特勒爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Botterell於大約1360年出生在英國史洛普爾阿斯頓 波特勒爾時，他的父親Thomas De Botterell27歲，母親Margaret Eyton27歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾阿斯頓 波特勒爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Botterell于大约1360年出生在英国史洛普爾阿斯頓 波特勒爾时，他的父亲Thomas De Botterell27岁，母亲Margaret Eyton27岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Botterell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Buttery
meanings: from Anglo-Norman French boterie ‘buttery’ (from Late Latin botaria, a derivative of bota ‘cask’), hence a metonymic occupational name for the keeper of a buttery. The term originally denoted a store for liquor but soon came to mean a store for provisions in general.

variants.name: Butter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Arno, Gerhard, Mathias, Reinhart.
Dutch and German: occupational name for a dairyman or seller of butter, from Middle Dutch butter and Middle High German buter.
English: occupational name for a dairyman, a shortened form of Middle English buterer ‘maker or seller of butter’. Alternatively, it may be a nickname from Middle English buter(e) ‘butter’.

variants.name: Botterill
meanings: 1 Norman, English: locative name from Les Bottereaux (Eure, Normandy), a place-name which apparently meant ‘the toads’. The surname is found in both a singular form Boterel and a plural form Boterels, Botereaus, which has developed to Buttress. Some of the post-medieval bearers may belong under (2).

2 Norman, English: occupational name from Anglo-Norman French * boterel, a diminutive form of Old French * boter ‘an officer who looks after the (wine) butts’. Both terms are derivatives of Old French butte, bote ‘wine-butt, cask’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Botterell was born about 1360, in Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas De Botterell, was 27 and his mother, Margaret Eyton, was 27. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Botterell est né environ 1360, à Aston Botterell, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas De Botterell, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Eyton, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Botterell um 1360 in Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas De Botterell 27 und seine Mutter Margaret Eyton 27. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Botterell è nato il circa 1360 nel Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas De Botterell, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Eyton, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Aston Botterell, Shropshire, England: イギリスシュロプシュアイアアストン ボッテレル
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Botterellが約1360年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアアストン ボッテレルで生まれた時，父Thomas De Botterellは27歳，母Margaret Eytonは27歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Botterell(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 슈럽실르 애스턴 벗테렐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas De Botterell(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Eyton(은)는 27세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Botterell nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Aston Botterell, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas De Botterell, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Eyton, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Botterell родился около 1360 г. в местности астон боттирэлл, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas De Botterell было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Eyton, 27. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Botterell nació aproximadamente 1360, en Aston Botterell, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas De Botterell, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margaret Eyton, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
